mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate tightens financial conditions and raises the equity discount rate, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are not significantly negative during months with rising FEDFUNDS (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates real discount rates and dampens corporate investment, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in real retail sales and an elevated federal funds rate create a double-drag on corporate revenues and financing costs, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days that exceed either factor alone.
failure_condition: The joint signal of weak RSAFS and elevated FEDFUNDS does not produce more negative 20-day returns than the sum of individual effects (interaction t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a slowdown in industrial production growth signals binding capacity constraints and peaking corporate profitability, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following INDPRO slowdowns in late-cycle expansions are not negative in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in nonfarm payrolls signals stronger aggregate income growth and reduces near-term recession probability, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 2000 thousand units indicate contraction in residential investment and construction employment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns following HOUST below 2000 are not significantly lower than unconditional returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in unemployment and consumer prices compress corporate profit margins through stagflationary cost pressures, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction of rising UNRATE and rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 20-day returns (regression interaction term p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Negative real GDP growth signals an economy-wide demand shortfall that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative GDPC1 prints are not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.
